title: Exceptions, Cleanup, and Context Managers · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Make the resource inventory survive invalid records, unavailable files, and partial operations. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a failure-resilient inventory importer. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Match exception handlers to the hierarchy and catch the narrow failures the current boundary can recover from.
Use try, except, else, and finally so protected work, success work, and cleanup have distinct roles.
Raise meaningful exceptions and preserve or suppress causes deliberately with exception chaining syntax.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-basics cumulative stakeholder program."""

from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Python Exception Hierarchy evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Python Exception Hierarchy assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Match exception handlers to the hierarchy and catch the narrow failures the current boundary can recover from. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: python errors resources. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Python Exception Hierarchy, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a failure-resilient inventory importer while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Catching Exception around an entire program is a reliable recovery strategy. This fails the Python Exception Hierarchy contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Python Failure Observability applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Log actionable context without secrets, preserve tracebacks, and distinguish user messages from operator evidence.
